channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Bank Union Seeks 5-Day Week, Links Demand To Austerity and Energy Savings
item.description: India's largest bank officers' union, AIBOC, has petitioned Prime Minister Modi for a five-day work week. The proposal links reduced working days to government austerity goals, projecting savings in fuel, energy, and reduced environmental impact, while arguing for enhanced operational efficiency.

item.title: IIFL Finance Surges 4.3% As Investors Dismiss ₹476 Crore Tax Demand
item.description: Shares of IIFL Finance climbed 4.31% to ₹464.25 on Wednesday, defying a ₹476 crore income tax demand notice. The company stated it possesses strong legal grounds to contest the demand. This positive stance, coupled with robust Q4 FY26 earnings showing a 148% profit surge and a 38% rise in Assets Under Management, appears to have alleviated investor concerns regarding the tax liability.

item.title: Redington Profit Plunges 41% Despite Revenue Surge; ₹6 Dividend Declared
item.description: Redington Ltd reported a 41.3% year-on-year drop in consolidated net profit to ₹391 crore for the fourth quarter, despite a robust 25.6% surge in revenue to ₹33,213 crore. The IT solutions provider recommended a final dividend of ₹6 per equity share, subject to shareholder approval. Strong performance was noted in India, with revenue up 50% and net profit up 41%.

item.title: Animoca's NUVA Links $19 Billion Tokenized Assets to Ethereum DeFi
item.description: Animoca Brands-backed NUVA has launched an Ethereum-based marketplace connecting approximately $19 billion in tokenized real-world assets from Figure Technologies Solutions' Provenance ecosystem. The platform aims to bridge institutional-grade assets like private credit and HELOCs into decentralized finance, offering retail investors access to yields previously unavailable.

item.title: Sagar Cements Rallies on ₹100 Cr Profit, Expansion Push
item.description: Sagar Cements reported a ₹100 crore net profit for the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026, a remarkable turnaround from a ₹73 lakh loss last year. Revenue surged 21% to ₹798 crore, driven by 8% volume growth and demand in infrastructure and rural segments. The company also advanced plans to amalgamate its subsidiary, Andhra Cements, and launch a new Superfine Building Materials division.

item.title: Clean Max Eyes 1500 MW Capacity Boost Amidst Soaring Corporate Green Energy Demand
item.description: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions is set to commission 1500 MW of renewable capacity this fiscal, fueled by robust corporate demand. The company reported adding 1400 MW last fiscal. Managing Director Kuldeep Jain highlighted that corporates are saving over 30% on costs and improving ESG scores by adopting greener power. This growth is independent of geopolitical events, driven by economics and sustainability.

item.title: TVS Motor Revs Up for Norton Motorcycle India Launch
item.description: TVS Motor Company is poised to introduce premium Norton motorcycles in India and globally by the second quarter of fiscal year 2027. This strategic push into the superbike segment is backed by significant investments in product development and manufacturing. The expansion aims to capitalize on demand across scooters, EVs, and premium bikes, while carefully monitoring potential commodity inflation and supply chain challenges.

item.title: LIC Housing Finance Profit Jumps 9%, Dividend Declared Amidst Revenue Dip
item.description: LIC Housing Finance reported a 9% year-over-year increase in consolidated net profit for the fiscal fourth quarter, reaching ₹1,493 crore. The company declared a ₹10 per share dividend. Despite this profit growth, total income saw a slight 1.3% downturn to ₹7,212 crore. The stock reacted positively, gaining 3.17%.

item.title: Ixigo Unveils AI-Native Travel App with Conversational Assistant
item.description: Ixigo has launched an AI-native travel app, revamping its AI assistant TARA and introducing agentic booking flows. This overhaul aims to transform trip planning into a more conversational and automated experience. The platform leverages AI from the ground up, featuring a multimodal assistant capable of handling voice and text queries in multiple languages for personalized recommendations and itinerary planning.
